statement.markdown:
Implement a function to merge two sorted linked lists into one sorted linked list.

Example:
Input: 
5 -> 3 -> 7 ->
2 -> 1 -> 4 ->

Output: 
2 -> 1 -> 3 -> 4 -> 5 -> 7 ->
statement.inputFormat: Two sorted linked lists represented as two head pointers.
statement.notes:
- Time complexity: O(n)
- Space complexity: O(1)
  where n is the total number of nodes in both linked lists.

constraints.description: Total number of nodes in both linked lists

editorial.title: Fixing the LinkedList Merge Function
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution iterates over both lists once.
commonMistakes: Incorrect implementation of the merge function

functionSignature.java: public boolean mergeTwoLists(ListNode list1, ListNode list2);

solutions.python:
class ListNode:
    def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
        self.val = val
        self.next = next

def mergeTwoLists(self, list1, list2):
    dummy = ListNode()
    curr = dummy
    while list1 and list2:
        if list1.val < list2.val:
            curr.next = list1
            list1 = list1.next
        else:
            curr.next = list2
            list2 = list2.next
        curr = curr.next
    curr.next = list1 or list2
    return dummy.next

# Read input from sys.stdin
nums1 = [int(i) for i in input().split(' ')]
nums1 = [ListNode(i) for i in nums1]
nums2 = [int(i) for i in input().split(' ')]
nums2 = [ListNode(i) for i in nums2]
# Call the function
list1 = nums1[0]
list2 = nums2[0]
ans = mergeTwoLists(list1, list2)

# Print the output
while ans:
    print(ans.val, end=' -> ')
    ans = ans.next

print('None')
